meta.title: Integration Loop: Replace with the dependency

lifecycle.note: Review producer and consumer packets before continuing the loop.

blocks.title: Replace with the producer and consumer goal
blocks.lede: A bounded producer/consumer loop for testing a dependency through real adoption and sending structured findings back upstream.

blocks.title: Loop endpoints
rows: Owns packets, cycle control, and stop conditions.

steps.title: Prepare dependency
steps.body: Producer emits version, source ref, tarball, or workspace link.

steps.title: Integrate
steps.body: Consumer adopts it in real product work and records evidence.

steps.title: Report
steps.body: Consumer emits blockers, logs, docs gaps, missing APIs, and proposed changes.

steps.title: Fix or defer
steps.body: Producer responds with accepted fixes, new ref, and retry instructions.

steps.title: Retry or close
steps.body: Consumer reruns against the new ref or the orchestrator closes the loop.

cycles.title: Cycle one
cycles.summary: Adopt the dependency in real product code and send findings back upstream.
cycles.nextStep: Run consumer verification and emit an integration report.

blocks.title: Consumer integration report
blocks.summary: Record tested version/source ref, install steps, touched areas, failing commands, logs, blockers, workarounds, and requested producer changes.

items.title: Consumer blocker one
items.body: Replace with blocker, reproduction, affected files, and requested upstream change.

blocks.title: Integration trust report
blocks.summary: Track which producer and consumer claims are grounded by cycle evidence.

claims.claim: The consumer tested the stated dependency version or source ref.
claims.notes: Update after the consumer run is complete.

blocks.title: Producer upstream response
blocks.response: Replace with accepted, rejected, deferred, or docs-only response.
blocks.nextStep: Replace with retry guidance or closeout condition.

blocks.title: Continue the loop?
blocks.prompt: Choose whether the next agent should retry, close, split, or block the integration cycle.
